documents.content: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell. It generates ATP through oxidative phosphorylation, converting nutrients into usable chemical energy.
Photosynthesis occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells. Chlorophyll absorbs sunlight and converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.
The French Revolution began in 1789 and led to the abolition of the French monarchy. Key causes included financial crisis, social inequality, and Enlightenment ideas.
The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles and was built to protect Chinese states from northern nomadic invasions. Construction began as early as the 7th century BC.

Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its readability and simplicity. It supports multiple programming paradigms including procedural, object-oriented, and functional programming.
Quicksort is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm with an average time complexity of O(n log n). It works by selecting a pivot and partitioning the array around it.
The Amazon River is the largest river in the world by discharge volume. It flows through South America, primarily Brazil, and drains into the Atlantic Ocean.
Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border, is the highest mountain on Earth at 8,849 meters above sea level.
Sourdough bread is made using a naturally fermented starter culture of wild yeast and lactic acid bacteria. The fermentation process creates the bread's characteristic tangy flavor.
A Bolognese sauce is a slow-cooked Italian meat sauce originating from Bologna. It typically includes ground beef, pork, onions, carrots, celery, tomatoes, and wine.
DNA replication is the process by which a cell copies its DNA before cell division. The double helix unwinds and each strand serves as a template for a new complementary strand.
Natural selection is the mechanism driving evolution proposed by Charles Darwin. Organisms with advantageous heritable traits survive and reproduce more successfully than others.
The Byzantine Empire was the continuation of the Eastern Roman Empire after the fall of Rome in 476 AD. Constantinople, modern-day Istanbul, served as its capital for over a thousand years.
The TCP/IP model defines how data is transmitted over the internet. It consists of four layers: the application layer, transport layer, internet layer, and network access layer.
Binary search is an efficient algorithm for finding an element in a sorted array. It repeatedly halves the search interval, achieving O(log n) time complexity.
The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert in the world, covering most of North Africa. It spans about 9.2 million square kilometers across 11 countries.
Croissants are a French pastry made from laminated dough, created by folding butter into the dough multiple times to produce hundreds of thin, flaky layers.
World War II began in 1939 when Germany invaded Poland. The conflict lasted until 1945 and involved most of the world's nations, resulting in an estimated 70-85 million deaths.

The human immune system defends the body against pathogens including bacteria, viruses, and fungi. It comprises innate immunity, which responds rapidly, and adaptive immunity, which is antigen-specific.
Recursion in programming is when a function calls itself directly or indirectly to solve a problem. Recursive solutions break a problem into smaller subproblems of the same type.
The Mississippi River is the second-longest river in North America, flowing approximately 3,730 km from its source in Minnesota to the Gulf of Mexico.
Ramen is a Japanese noodle dish consisting of wheat noodles served in a meat or fish-based broth, typically flavored with soy sauce or miso, and topped with sliced pork, nori, and green onions.
The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the late 18th century. It marked the transition from hand production to machine manufacturing, driven by steam power and new factory systems.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene-editing technology derived from a bacterial immune system. It allows scientists to precisely cut and modify DNA sequences in living organisms.
Hash tables store key-value pairs and provide average O(1) time complexity for insertion, deletion, and lookup. They use a hash function to map keys to array indices.
The Andes mountain range runs along the western coast of South America. At over 7,000 km, it is the longest continental mountain range in the world.
Neural networks are machine learning models inspired by the structure of biological brains. They consist of layers of interconnected nodes, or neurons, that transform inputs into outputs.
The Silk Road was an ancient network of trade routes connecting China with Central Asia, the Middle East, and Europe. It facilitated the exchange of goods, ideas, and cultures for centuries.
Fermentation is a metabolic process in which microorganisms such as yeast or bacteria convert sugars into alcohol, gases, or acids under anaerobic conditions.
The HTTP protocol is the foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web. HTTP/2 introduced multiplexing, header compression, and server push to improve web performance.

queries.text: efficient sorting algorithm divide and conquer
largest river by water volume South America
baking bread with wild yeast fermentation
how DNA copies itself before cell division
Darwin evolution survival of the fittest
hash map data structure constant time lookup
